selection_policy: Includes the 79 previously published high-confidence cases plus 10 high-confidence cases from the 20260719T010350Z recurring update; excludes every deferred, unsure, dropped, unsupported, duplicate, promotional, or unverifiable candidate.

items.title: Build a Voxel Pod Racer From One Prompt
items.takeaway: Use a short scene concept to prototype a playable voxel pod-racing experience, then plan a second pass for racers, a finish line, and detail review.
items.body_notes: The creator reports that Kimi K3 produced version one from a single public prompt and says version two would add more racers, a finish line, and detail review.
items.decision_reason: Concrete generated artifact, exact public prompt, visible demo media, and an explicit next iteration boundary.
items.prompt_boundary: The exact reusable prompt is present in the supplied source.

items.title: Compare Frogger Builds With the Same Prompt

items.body_notes: The creator shares a Kimi K3 Frogger version as a same-prompt comparison between two models. The supplied source does not publish the prompt text or evaluation rubric.
items.decision_reason: A controlled same-prompt comparison with visible output and a clear missing-rubric caveat.
items.prompt_boundary: The source says the prompt was the same but does not publish it; omit the prompt block.

items.title: Benchmark Kimi Code on Coding Agents
items.takeaway: Compare Kimi K3 across multiple coding-agent suites and per-task cost before treating a single frontend leaderboard as general coding-agent performance.
items.body_notes: Artificial Analysis reports Kimi K3 in Kimi Code CLI at 57 on its Coding Agent Index, tied for fifth overall. The post breaks the result into Terminal-Bench v2 at 84%, DeepSWE at 64%, and SWE-Atlas-QnA at 23%, with an average reported cost of $3.18 per task and named comparisons against other frontier models.
items.decision_reason: Named coding-agent index with task-suite scores, peer comparisons, per-task cost, and image evidence.
items.prompt_boundary: The post reports aggregate benchmark results; individual benchmark tasks and prompts are not reproduced.

items.title: Configure Kimi as a Codex Subagent
items.takeaway: Keep Codex responsible for orchestration and delegate frontend execution to a Kimi K3 OpenCode subagent with an explicit secret-handling boundary.
items.body_notes: nauczymycieAI publishes a three-stage setup: install and verify OpenCode, create a Kimi API key manually without pasting it into Codex, connect OpenCode to Kimi K3, and create a global Codex skill that routes frontend, UI, layout, presentation, and animation tasks to the subagent while Codex keeps logic and orchestration responsibility.
items.decision_reason: Detailed Codex and OpenCode subagent tutorial with explicit roles, setup steps, secret-handling boundary, and image evidence.

In that separate harness Kimi completed 53 of 67 attempts, or 79%, and ranked seventh, illustrating how evaluation format changes the conclusion.
